perturbation.original_text: 7. Export Control. Discloser has categori zed this project as export controlled under the International Traffic in Arms Regulation (ITAR ) or the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) and the applicable Export Control Regulations of the United States. As a result, certain technical data, hardware, software or other information furnished to Recipient shall be labeled “Export Controlled.” Other specific technical information created by the Recipient may be export controlled as well if it relates to the specific produc t design or prototype to be created in this project. Recipient agrees that export controlled information shall not be disclosed to any unauthorized foreign person, firm, country, incl uding foreign persons employed by Discloser. Recipient understands there are civil and criminal penalties for failure to comply with U.S. Export Control laws and will comply with all applicable laws while performing services for Discloser. Specifically, Recipient agrees to comply with U.S. export control laws and regulations on all information it receives that is labeled “Export Controlled” and on information it creates in the scope of work on this project that is export c ontrolled. Recipient further warrants that all individuals that will be given access to this info rmation are U.S. citizens or permanent U.S. residents with a valid permanent resident cards, and that the information disclosed to Recipient will be stored securely. If Recipient has any qu estions concerning this clause, Recipient should contact Discloser immediately.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Counterparts. This Agreement may be ex ecuted in counterparts, each of which will be deemed an original, and all of which constitute one and the same instrument. Each Party will execute and promptly deliver to the other Party a copy of this Agreement bearing an Original Signature. “Original Signature” means a copy of a signature of a Party that is reproduced or transmitted via email or a .pdf file, photocopy, facsimile, or other process of complete and accurate reproduction and transmission. 7. Export Control. Discloser has categori zed this project as export controlled under the International Traffic in Arms Regulation (ITAR ) or the Export Administration Regulations (EAR) and the applicable Export Control Regulations of the United States. As a result, certain technical data, hardware, software or other information furnished to Recipient shall be labeled “Export Controlled.” Other specific technical information created by the Recipient may be export controlled as well if it relates to the specific produc t design or prototype to be created in this project. Recipient agrees that export controlled information shall not be disclosed to any unauthorized foreign person, firm, country, incl uding foreign persons employed by Discloser. Recipient understands there are civil and criminal penalties for failure to comply with U.S. Export Control laws and will comply with all applicable laws while performing services for Discloser. Specifically, Recipient agrees to comply with U.S. export control laws and regulations on all information it receives that is labeled “Export Controlled” and on information it creates in the scope of work on this project that is export c ontrolled. Recipient further warrants that all individuals that will be given access to this info rmation are U.S. citizens or permanent U.S. residents with a valid permanent resident cards, and that the information disclosed to Recipient will be stored securely. If Recipient has any qu estions concerning this clause, Recipient should contact Discloser immediately.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Export Control' clause into the 'Counterparts' section buries critical compliance information within a section typically dealing with administrative matters. This placement reduces the likelihood that the Recipient will fully understand and adhere to export control regulations.
perturbation.law_explanation: 22 CFR § 123.22(b) requires that licenses for exports include conditions and limitations. By placing the export control clause in a section that does not directly relate to export control, the agreement may be deemed to have not sufficiently informed the recipient about the 'conditions and limitations' applicable to the export of controlled items, including restrictions on disclosure to foreign persons.

perturbation.justification: The structural flaw of moving the 'Export Control' clause into the 'Counterparts' section creates a legal contradiction. The Law Explanation states that 22 CFR § 123.22(b) requires that licenses for exports include conditions and limitations. By burying the export control clause, the agreement may be deemed to have not sufficiently informed the recipient about the 'conditions and limitations' applicable to the export of controlled items. This is a direct contradiction as it hinders clear communication of export conditions, potentially violating the requirement for explicit conditions on export licenses.
